_doc: Labeled recurring-defect corpus (WS0.1). Each label asserts a gate's verdict over a run: expect 'flag' (a confirmed defect must be caught) or 'no-flag' (a confirmed false-positive must NOT fire). status 'enforced' gates CI; 'pending' documents a target for a not-yet-built gate (skipped, never fails). corpus 'fixtures' = committed hermetic dirs (always enforced); corpus 'generated-stories' = live archived runs (enforced when present, skipped when gitignored-absent). gate = a name from scripts/replay-gates.ts REGISTRY.

labels.note: dead condition accepted_victor_invitation vs setter received_victor_invitation + write-only residue (mirrors g17 s3-2)
FP guard: a flag set AND read must not trip the contract validator
FP guard: clean prose must not trip the scaffold/duplication detector
g17 dropped the s1-1 Sadie cold-open + climbing blog-counter plants
g17 accepted_victor_invitation read but never set; 49 write-only residue flags
documents that the deterministic detector does NOT catch g17's verbatim payoff-beat duplication (gap WS1.7 closes via a dedicated dedup pass)
WS0.3: encounter outcome storylets narrate the protagonist in 3rd person (detected; deterministic backstop coerces to 2nd person, residue routes to regen)

WS1.8: g17 pronoun warnings are false positives (oblique pronoun = a different person). Deterministic guards (protagonist-referent, two-actor) cut them 17->15 but the residue is coreference-hard — zeroing needs the LLM coreference judge. Stays advisory/pending until that lands.
WS1.2 target: blog counter regressed 91,428 -> 50,001 and was never caught
